title: Improving lab-of-origin prediction of genetically engineered plasmids via deep metric learning
data_sources: Custom chimeric sequence (Alley et al., GitHub altLabs)

data_scope.sequence_encoding: BPE vocabulary of 1001 tokens, max length 1000
data_scope.phenotype_features: growth strain, temperature, copy number, host species, bacterial resistance, selectable markers

core_conclusion: A deep metric learning (triplet network) approach achieves 90.4% top-10 accuracy in predicting the lab of origin of engineered plasmids from the Addgene repository (81,834 sequences, 1,314 classes), a 5.4-point improvement over prior state of the art. The joint embedding space for DNA sequences and labs enables clustering, few-shot learning (76% top-10 accuracy with 10% of training data), and interpretable extraction of lab-specific design signatures via integrated gradients.
why_it_matters: Sets a new accuracy benchmark for genetic engineering attribution with forensic utility; unlocks a reusable joint embedding space for clustering and similarity search; enables attribution of rare labs via few-shot learning without retraining; provides ground-truth-free sequence-level interpretability; demonstrates robustness to up to 800 random point mutations.
why_nature: Genuine methodological transfer of triplet/metric learning from computer vision to computational biology; broad societal impact via biosecurity and responsible biotechnology; platform-style contribution combining attribution, clustering, FSL, and interpretability; fully reproducible via Code Ocean open capsule.
added_value_to_my_research: DNA foundation-model encoder as drop-in for BPE layer mirrors my practice of fine-tuning geospatial foundation models for domain tasks
NTI attribution audit vs. biological annotation parallels my XAI workflow of comparing SHAP/MGWR coefficients against geographic theory
Hierarchical entity embeddings (lab → institution → country) map onto nested spatial scales in R2SFCA facility-network accessibility
FSL active learning loop mirrors spatiotemporal active learning for expensive ground-truth collection in mobile-phone/GPS data
Spatial stylometry via triplet embeddings could attribute geospatial data products to producing teams, supporting open science/FAIR provenance

FSL results: top-10 accuracy vs. training fraction (1 shot to 90%) and histogram of lab-of-origin rank using a single representative plasmid
FSL top-10 accuracy for rare labs (2-10 plasmids only) — performance drop for the smallest labs
2D t-SNE of triplet vs. softmax feature spaces; robustness to 1-1000 random point mutations on the triplet model
t-SNE of test-set plasmids colored by model agreement — hard cases concentrate in embedding high-density core
Normalized token importance (NTI) via integrated gradients: global average, David Root lab, difference from global, and most-distant lab
